title: Amazing Grace
attribution: Trad. American melody 'New Britain'; words John Newton (1725-1807). Public domain. ABC: John Chambers collection.
blurb: A grace that saved a wretch like me.

title: Auld Lang Syne
attribution: Trad. Scots (Burns, 1788), melody 'Can Ye Labour Lea'. Public domain. ABC after R. L. Walker (John Chambers collection).
blurb: Should auld acquaintance be forgot.

title: Danny Boy
attribution: Trad. Irish 'Londonderry Air'; 'Danny Boy' words F. Weatherly (1910). Public domain. ABC: John Chambers collection.
blurb: The beloved Londonderry Air.

title: Deck the Hall
attribution: Old Welsh Air ("Nos Galan"); ABC from "The Everyday Song Book" (1927), transcribed by John Chambers, trillian.mit.edu
blurb: A merry Welsh Christmas carol full of fa-la-la-las!

title: Frere Jacques
attribution: Traditional French round. Public domain. ABC from folkinfo.org (Singing Together, BBC Publications, 1951), via abcnotation.com.
blurb: A round about brother John sleeping through the morning bells!

title: Für Elise
attribution: Beethoven, Bagatelle in A minor 'Für Elise' (1810). Public domain. ABC transcription (F. Nordberg, public domain).
blurb: One of the most famous tunes ever.

title: Greensleeves
attribution: Traditional English (c. 1580). Public domain. ABC from The Session (CC0).
blurb: A haunting Tudor melody.

title: Happy Birthday
composer: Patty S. Hill and Mildred J. Hill
attribution: Melody by Patty S. Hill and Mildred J. Hill (public domain since 2016); ABC from John Chambers collection, trillian.mit.edu (thisoldtractor mirror)
blurb: The classic tune everyone sings when it's your special day!

title: Jingle Bells
attribution: James Lord Pierpont, 'Jingle Bells' (1857). Public domain. ABC from the John Chambers collection (trillian.mit.edu).
blurb: The jolliest sleigh-ride song of them all!

title: Lightly row
attribution: German folk tune. Public domain. ABC transcribed by Frank Nordberg, musicaviva.com, via abcnotation.com.
blurb: Glide across the deep blue sea in this gentle little boat song!

title: London Bridge
attribution: Traditional English game song, "The Everyday Song Book" (1927). Public domain. ABC transcribed by John Chambers (trillian.mit.edu), via abcnotation.com.
blurb: London Bridge is falling down, my fair lady!

title: Mary Had a Little Lamb
attribution: Traditional (England); ABC from musicaviva.com via John Chambers collection (trillian.mit.edu)
blurb: Mary's fluffy little lamb follows her everywhere she goes.

title: Michael Row the Boat Ashore
attribution: Traditional African-American spiritual. Public domain. ABC transcribed by Frank Nordberg (musicaviva.com), via the John Chambers collection.
blurb: Row the boat ashore, hallelujah!

title: Minuet in G Major
attribution: Petzold, Minuet in G (BWV Anh. 114), formerly attr. J. S. Bach. Public domain. ABC ed. AW (SPUDS).
blurb: An elegant dance from Bach's notebook.

title: Molly Malone
attribution: Trad. Irish ('Cockles and Mussels'). Public domain. ABC from O'Neill's Music of Ireland (1903) via V. Brennan.
blurb: In Dublin's fair city...

title: Ode to Joy
attribution: Beethoven, 'Ode to Joy' (Symphony No. 9, 1824). Public domain. ABC from the John Chambers collection.
blurb: The happy tune everyone knows!

title: Oh Susanna
attribution: Stephen C. Foster, 'Oh! Susanna' (1848). Public domain. ABC transcribed by Frank Nordberg (musicaviva.com), via the John Chambers collection.
blurb: Stephen Foster's sunny song about coming from Alabama!

title: Old MacDonald Had A Farm
attribution: Traditional English. Public domain. ABC from Paul Hardy's Songs Tunebook (pghardy.net, CC BY-NC-SA), via abcnotation.com.
blurb: Meet all the noisy animals on the farm, E-I-E-I-O!

title: Row, Row, Row Your Boat
attribution: Traditional round; ABC from John Chambers collection (trillian.mit.edu)
blurb: Row your boat gently down the stream, merrily on your way.

title: Scarborough Fair
attribution: Trad. English ballad. Public domain. ABC transcr. Frank Nordberg (musicaviva.com).
blurb: Parsley, sage, rosemary and thyme.

title: This Old Man
attribution: Traditional. Public domain. ABC transcribed by Frank Nordberg (musicaviva.com), via the John Chambers collection.
blurb: This old man, he played one, knick-knack on your thumb!

title: Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star
attribution: Traditional kids' song; ABC from John Chambers collection (trillian.mit.edu, R.L. Walker abc/twinkle)
blurb: A sparkly little tune about a star up in the night sky.

title: We Wish You a Merry Christmas
attribution: Trad. English carol (West Country). Public domain. ABC via folkinfo.org.
blurb: And a happy new year!

title: When the saints go marching in
attribution: American spiritual, traditional. Public domain. ABC transcribed by Frank Nordberg, musicaviva.com, via abcnotation.com.
blurb: A toe-tapping march you'll want to sing along to!

title: Yankee Doodle
attribution: Traditional (c. 1730). Public domain. ABC from the John Chambers collection (trillian.mit.edu).
blurb: A bouncy marching tune from way back!

title: Canon in D
attribution: Pachelbel, Canon in D (c. 1680). Public domain. MIDI: mfiles.co.uk.
blurb: The wedding favourite.

title: Rondo alla Turca
attribution: Mozart, Rondo alla Turca from Sonata K.331 (1783). Public domain. MIDI: mfiles.co.uk.
blurb: The dazzling Turkish March.
